questions.q: Pulakeshin II was the greatest ruler of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II was the most celebrated king of the Chalukya dynasty of Badami.

questions.q: In which century did Pulakeshin II reign?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II ruled in the 7th century CE.

questions.q: Which northern emperor did Pulakeshin II famously halt at the Narmada river?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II stopped Harsha's southern advance at the Narmada, a celebrated achievement.

questions.q: The Chalukya dynasty of Pulakeshin II was based in which city?
questions.explanation: The dynasty was based at Badami (ancient Vatapi) in present-day Karnataka.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's court received an embassy from a ruler of which empire?
questions.explanation: His fame drew an embassy from the Sasanian Persian king, attesting to his renown.

questions.q: The Chalukyas under Pulakeshin II ruled mainly over which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: The Badami Chalukyas dominated the Deccan in peninsular India.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II is best described by which role in Indian history?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II was a powerful warrior-king who expanded Chalukya dominance.

questions.q: The 'II' in Pulakeshin II indicates what?
options: He was the second ruler of that name in his line
questions.explanation: He was the second Chalukya king named Pulakeshin in his dynasty.

questions.q: Badami, the Chalukya capital, lies in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Badami is located in the modern state of Karnataka.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's victory over Harsha is significant because it stopped the spread of which power into the south?
questions.explanation: By defeating Harsha, Pulakeshin II checked the expansion of a north Indian empire into the Deccan.

questions.q: The chief source detailing Pulakeshin II's exploits is which inscription, composed by his court poet?
questions.explanation: The Aihole prashasti, composed by the poet Ravikirti, records Pulakeshin II's achievements.

questions.q: Who composed the famous Aihole inscription praising Pulakeshin II?
questions.explanation: The Jain poet Ravikirti composed the Aihole prashasti in 634 CE.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's main southern rival, whom he fought repeatedly, was which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas of Kanchipuram were Pulakeshin II's principal rivals in the south.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II came to the throne after a struggle against which usurping relative?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II seized power after a civil conflict with his uncle Mangalesha.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II appointed his brother Kubja Vishnuvardhana as viceroy of which region, founding a new branch?
questions.explanation: His brother Kubja Vishnuvardhana became viceroy of Vengi, founding the Eastern Chalukya line.

questions.q: The Sasanian Persian ruler whose embassy is associated with Pulakeshin II's court was named what?
questions.explanation: The embassy is traditionally linked to the Sasanian emperor Khusrau (Khosrow) II.

questions.q: A painting at which famous cave site is sometimes interpreted as depicting Pulakeshin II receiving the Persian embassy?
questions.explanation: A mural in Cave 1 at Ajanta is often interpreted as showing the reception of a Persian embassy at the Chalukya court.

questions.q: The title Pulakeshin II assumed reflecting his supremacy in the Deccan was which?
questions.explanation: After defeating Harsha, Pulakeshin II styled himself Dakshinapatheshvara, lord of the south.

questions.q: The Chinese pilgrim Xuanzang, visiting the Deccan, left an account praising the kingdom of which ruler, generally identified with Pulakeshin II?
questions.explanation: Xuanzang admired the people and king of Maharashtra, generally identified with Pulakeshin II's realm.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's reign ended tragically when his capital Badami was captured by which Pallava king around 642 CE?
questions.explanation: The Pallava king Narasimhavarman I captured and sacked Badami around 642 CE, defeating Pulakeshin II.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's father, a Chalukya king who began the dynasty's rise, was named what?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II was the son of Kirtivarman I, who had strengthened the early Chalukya state.

questions.q: In his early campaigns Pulakeshin II subdued the Kadambas of which capital?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II defeated the Kadambas, whose capital was Banavasi, during his expansion.

questions.q: The Aihole inscription is dated to which year, providing a firm chronological anchor for Pulakeshin II?
questions.explanation: The Aihole prashasti is dated to 634 CE (Saka 556), anchoring Pulakeshin II's chronology.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II defeated a confederation of the Gangas and which other power in the early phase of his reign in southern Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II overcame neighbouring powers including the Gangas and the Alupas in the south.

questions.q: On his eastern campaigns Pulakeshin II is recorded as having subdued rulers along the coast and crossed which major river region?
questions.explanation: His eastern conquests extended over the Godavari-Krishna region, leading to the Vengi viceroyalty.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's clash with the Pallava king Mahendravarman I is recorded near which Pallava stronghold during his southern campaign?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II defeated Mahendravarman I in a battle near Pullalur, close to the Pallava capital Kanchipuram.

questions.q: The Chalukya capital Badami was known by which Sanskrit name appearing in inscriptions?
questions.explanation: Badami was called Vatapi in inscriptions, and Narasimhavarman I took the title Vatapikonda for conquering it.

questions.q: Pulakeshin II's standing in the Deccan is sometimes contrasted with which contemporary dynasty he failed to fully subdue in the northwest Deccan, the Latas, Malavas and Gurjaras of the region being made tributaries?
options: He was defeated by the Latas
He reduced the Latas, Malavas, and Gurjaras to feudatory status
He allied with the Gurjaras against Harsha
He never campaigned northwest of Badami
questions.explanation: The Aihole inscription claims Pulakeshin II made the Latas, Malavas, and Gurjaras his feudatories.

questions.q: Chalukya power was eventually restored after Pulakeshin II's death by his son, who recaptured Badami and was named what?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II's son Vikramaditya I revived Chalukya fortunes and recaptured Badami from the Pallavas.

questions.q: The Aihole inscription's poet Ravikirti compared his own literary skill to which two classical Sanskrit poets?
questions.explanation: Ravikirti boastfully likened his poetic fame to that of Kalidasa and Bharavi in the Aihole prashasti.
